assumptions: A single local user runs the tracker from a terminal.

decisions.title: Node.js CLI runtime
decisions.context: The tracker must run without a server or browser.
decisions.decision: Ship a Node.js CLI with local JSON persistence.
consequences: Node.js 22 or newer is required to run the tracker.

decisions.title: CSV export
decisions.decision: Render reports as text and CSV from one model.
consequences: Report rendering gains a second output writer.

phases.name: Local tracker core
phases.objective: Store and list tasks from the command line.

phases.name: Reporting
phases.objective: Summarize tracked work in text and CSV.
exitCriteria: A weekly summary renders from stored tasks.

tasks.title: Persist tasks to a local JSON store
tasks.objective: Create, read, and update tasks on disk atomically.

tasks.title: Add the add and list commands
tasks.objective: Expose the store through the CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: add and list round-trip a task.

tasks.title: Render weekly summaries as text and CSV
tasks.objective: Summarize completed tasks for a date range in both formats.
acceptanceCriteria: The summary counts tasks by status.

risks.description: Concurrent writes could corrupt the JSON store.
risks.mitigation: Write through a temporary file and rename atomically.
